model.name: recipientList
model.title: Recipient List
model.description: Route messages to a number of dynamically specified recipients
model.label: eip,routing

id.description: Sets the id of this node

note.description: Sets the note of this node

description.description: Sets the description of this node

disabled.label: advanced
disabled.description: Disables this EIP from the route.

expression.description: Expression that returns which endpoints (url) to send the message to (the recipients). If the expression return an empty value then the message is not sent to any recipients.

delimiter.description: Delimiter used if the Expression returned multiple endpoints. Can be turned off using the value false.

aggregationStrategy.description: Sets the AggregationStrategy to be used to assemble the replies from the recipients, into a single outgoing message.

aggregationStrategyMethodName.label: advanced
aggregationStrategyMethodName.description: This option can be used to explicitly declare the method name to use, when using POJOs as the AggregationStrategy.

aggregationStrategyMethodAllowNull.label: advanced
aggregationStrategyMethodAllowNull.description: If this option is false then the aggregate method is not used if there was no data to enrich. If this option is true then null values is used as the oldExchange (when no data to enrich), when using POJOs as the AggregationStrategy.

parallelAggregate.label: advanced
parallelAggregate.description: If enabled then the aggregate method on AggregationStrategy can be called concurrently. Notice that this would require the implementation of AggregationStrategy to be implemented as thread-safe. By default this is false meaning that Camel synchronizes the call to the aggregate method. Though in some use-cases this can be used to archive higher performance when the AggregationStrategy is implemented as thread-safe.

parallelProcessing.description: If enabled then sending messages to the recipients occurs concurrently. Note the caller thread will still wait until all messages has been fully processed, before it continues.

synchronous.description: Sets whether synchronous processing should be strictly used. When enabled then the same thread is used to continue routing after the recipient list is complete, even if parallel processing is enabled.

timeout.description: Sets a total timeout specified in millis, when using parallel processing. If the Recipient List hasn't been able to send and process all replies within the given timeframe, then the timeout triggers and the Recipient List breaks out and continues.

executorService.label: advanced
executorService.description: Refers to a custom Thread Pool to be used for parallel processing. Notice if you set this option, then parallel processing is automatically implied, and you do not have to enable that option as well.

stopOnException.label: advanced
stopOnException.description: Will now stop further processing if an exception or failure occurred during processing of an exchange and the caused exception will be thrown. Will also stop if processing the exchange failed (has a fault message) or an exception was thrown and handled by the error handler (such as using onException).

ignoreInvalidEndpoints.label: advanced
ignoreInvalidEndpoints.description: Ignore the invalidate endpoint exception when try to create a producer with that endpoint.

streaming.label: advanced
streaming.description: If enabled then Camel will process replies out-of-order, eg in the order they come back. If disabled, Camel will process replies in the same order as defined by the recipient list.

onPrepare.label: advanced
onPrepare.description: Uses the Processor when preparing the exchange to be sent. This can be used to deep-clone messages that should be sent, or any custom logic needed before the exchange is sent.

cacheSize.label: advanced
cacheSize.description: Sets the maximum size used by the ProducerCache which is used to cache and reuse producers when uris are reused. Use 0 for default cache size, or -1 to turn cache off.

shareUnitOfWork.label: advanced
shareUnitOfWork.description: Shares the UnitOfWork with the parent and each of the sub messages. Recipient List will by default not share unit of work between the parent exchange and each recipient exchange. This means each sub exchange has its own individual unit of work.

CamelRecipientListEndpoint.label: producer
CamelRecipientListEndpoint.description: The endpoint uri of this recipient list

CamelToEndpoint.label: producer
CamelToEndpoint.description: Endpoint URI where this Exchange is being sent to
